title: Dermatomyositis: treat with systemic glucocorticoids
vignette: A 62-year-old woman comes to the office due to a 3-month history of progressive muscle weakness. The patient has been having difficulty with activities such as getting out of the car and carrying groceries from the store. She has also developed a rash on her face and hands. The patient has a history of well-controlled hypertension and does not use tobacco, alcohol, or illicit drugs. Vital signs are within normal limits. Physical examination shows weakness of shoulder abduction and hip flexion. Strength of the distal limb muscles and deep tendon reflexes are normal. Skin examination shows violaceous papules over the knuckles (Gottron papules) and a violaceous rash over the upper eyelids (heliotrope rash). Which of the following is the most appropriate initial treatment for this patient's current condition?

clues.note: Subacute, progressive weakness over months suggests an inflammatory myopathy (dermatomyositis/polymyositis) rather than an acute or purely neurologic process.

clues.text: getting out of the car and carrying groceries
clues.note: Difficulty with rising and lifting reflects PROXIMAL muscle weakness (hip/shoulder girdle) - the pattern of myopathy, not distal neuropathy.

clues.text: rash on her face and hands
clues.note: A myopathy PLUS a characteristic rash points specifically to DERMATOMYOSITIS (distinguishing it from polymyositis, which lacks skin findings).

clues.text: weakness of shoulder abduction and hip flexion
clues.note: Symmetric proximal weakness (shoulder/hip girdle) is the hallmark distribution of inflammatory myopathy.

clues.text: Strength of the distal limb muscles and deep tendon reflexes are normal
clues.note: Preserved distal strength and intact reflexes argue against neuropathy or motor neuron disease, confirming a myopathic (muscle) process.

clues.text: violaceous papules over the knuckles (Gottron papules) and a violaceous rash over the upper eyelids (heliotrope rash)
clues.note: Gottron papules (violaceous papules over the knuckles) and a heliotrope rash (violaceous discoloration of the eyelids) are PATHOGNOMONIC cutaneous signs of dermatomyositis - they clinch the diagnosis.

whatMatters: Proximal symmetric weakness + Gottron papules + heliotrope rash = DERMATOMYOSITIS. Initial treatment is systemic GLUCOCORTICOIDS (e.g., prednisone). Also screen for an underlying occult malignancy (paraneoplastic association, especially adenocarcinoma). Answer D.
strategy: Pin the diagnosis from the muscle pattern + skin signs, then pick first-line therapy. Dermatomyositis = proximal weakness + Gottron papules/heliotrope rash; treat with systemic glucocorticoids. Reject treatments tied to the distractor diagnoses (AChE inhibitor = myasthenia, copper = deficiency myeloneuropathy, riluzole = ALS, levothyroxine = hypothyroid myopathy).

eliminations.label: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitors - myasthenia gravis
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: drugs like pyridostigmine boost ACh at the neuromuscular junction in myasthenia gravis. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: myasthenia causes FATIGABLE, fluctuating ocular/bulbar weakness WITHOUT a rash - not fixed proximal weakness with Gottron papules/heliotrope rash. ✅ Correct if: ptosis, diplopia, fatigable weakness improving with rest.

eliminations.label: Copper supplementation - copper deficiency myeloneuropathy
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: replaces copper in deficiency (often post-bariatric or high zinc intake). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: copper deficiency causes anemia, neutropenia, and a myeloneuropathy with hypopigmentation - not an inflammatory myopathy with heliotrope rash. ✅ Correct if: anemia + neutropenia + sensory ataxia with low copper.

eliminations.label: Riluzole therapy - amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: a glutamate inhibitor that modestly slows ALS. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: ALS gives mixed upper and lower motor neuron signs (hyperreflexia, fasciculations, bulbar dysfunction) and NO rash; this patient has a pure myopathy with normal reflexes and a pathognomonic rash. ✅ Correct if: progressive UMN + LMN signs without sensory loss or rash.

eliminations.label: Thyroid hormone replacement - hypothyroid myopathy
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: levothyroxine for hypothyroidism. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: hypothyroid myopathy causes proximal weakness but with prominent muscle PAIN/cramps and other hypothyroid features (weight gain, cold intolerance) - not Gottron papules and a heliotrope rash. ✅ Correct if: proximal weakness with elevated CK plus classic hypothyroid signs and high TSH.

reasoning: Subacute proximal symmetric weakness (shoulder/hip) with normal distal strength and reflexes = inflammatory myopathy
Gottron papules + heliotrope rash specify DERMATOMYOSITIS
First-line treatment is systemic glucocorticoids (e.g., prednisone)
Also evaluate for occult malignancy (paraneoplastic); answer D
mechanism: Dermatomyositis is an autoimmune inflammatory myopathy that combines symmetric PROXIMAL muscle weakness with characteristic cutaneous findings - Gottron papules (violaceous papules over the knuckles/bony prominences) and a heliotrope rash (violaceous eyelid discoloration). Muscle injury elevates creatine kinase and aldolase; autoantibodies (ANA, anti-Jo-1) are often positive, and biopsy shows perifascicular inflammation and atrophy with perimysial/perivascular involvement. Initial treatment is systemic GLUCOCORTICOIDS (e.g., prednisone), frequently with steroid-sparing immunosuppressants. Because dermatomyositis can be paraneoplastic (notably with adenocarcinomas), affected patients should be evaluated for an underlying occult malignancy.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Rheum > dermatomyositis: proximal weakness + Gottron papules + heliotrope rash; elevated CK/aldolase; ANA, anti-Jo-1; perifascicular atrophy; treat with glucocorticoids; screen for malignancy.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The stem asks for INITIAL TREATMENT. Diagnose dermatomyositis from weakness pattern + rash, then choose first-line therapy (glucocorticoids).

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on Gottron papules, heliotrope rash, proximal vs distal weakness, perifascicular, anti-Jo-1, or paraneoplastic? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: Other myopathy/weakness treatments tempt only if you misdiagnose - lock the rash (Gottron/heliotrope) = dermatomyositis, and glucocorticoids follow.

difficulty.label: 🖼️ Imaging / diagram
difficulty.message: The skin photo shows Gottron papules over the knuckles and a heliotrope eyelid rash. If you couldn't read it, the stem already says 'rash on face and hands' with proximal weakness - enough to diagnose dermatomyositis.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank: dermatomyositis = proximal weakness + Gottron/heliotrope, treat with glucocorticoids, screen for cancer; myasthenia = AChE inhibitors; ALS = riluzole; hypothyroid myopathy = levothyroxine.

keywords.front: Proximal weakness + Gottron papules + heliotrope rash

keywords.back: Paraneoplastic association - screen for cancer (esp. adenocarcinoma)
Violaceous discoloration of the upper eyelids (dermatomyositis)

firstAid.detail: Rheum > proximal weakness + Gottron/heliotrope; CK/aldolase up; anti-Jo-1; glucocorticoids; malignancy screen.
firstAid.location: Rheum > proximal weakness + Gottron/heliotrope; CK/aldolase up; anti-Jo-1; glucocorticoids; malignancy screen.

firstAid.detail: Neuro/Rheum > myasthenia (AChE inhibitor), ALS (riluzole), hypothyroid myopathy (levothyroxine), copper deficiency.
firstAid.location: Neuro/Rheum > myasthenia (AChE inhibitor), ALS (riluzole), hypothyroid myopathy (levothyroxine), copper deficiency.

firstAid.detail: Rheum > both proximal weakness; DM adds skin findings + perifascicular atrophy; PM endomysial CD8 T cells.
firstAid.location: Rheum > both proximal weakness; DM adds skin findings + perifascicular atrophy; PM endomysial CD8 T cells.

memoryHook: Heliotrope eyelids + Gottron knuckles + can't-lift-groceries weakness = dermatomyositis. Start steroids - and go hunting for a hidden cancer.
